title: The Starry Night
description_narrative: A swirling, dynamic night sky over a quiet village, painted during Van Gogh's stay at the Saint-Paul-de-Mausole asylum in Saint-Rémy-de-Provence. It is one of the most recognized masterpieces in the history of Western art.
tags: night, sky, stars, village, post-impressionism, landscape

title: A Sunday on La Grande Jatte
description_narrative: A monumental example of Pointillism, depicting relaxing Parisians at a park on the banks of the River Seine. Seurat's technique of using distinct dots of color that blend in the viewer's eye fundamentally altered the direction of modern art.
tags: park, people, pointillism, river, leisure, sunday

title: The Great Wave off Kanagawa
medium: Polychrome woodblock print; ink and color on paper
description_narrative: An enormous rogue wave threatening three boats off the coast of the town of Kanagawa, while Mount Fuji rises in the background. It is Hokusai's most famous work and one of the most recognizable works of Japanese art in the world.
tags: wave, ocean, boats, mount fuji, ukiyo-e, landscape

title: Wanderer above the Sea of Fog
description_narrative: A quintessential piece of Romanticism. A man stands upon a rocky precipice with his back to the viewer, gazing out over a landscape covered in a thick sea of fog. It evokes a feeling of the sublime and the profound scale of nature.
tags: fog, mountains, romanticism, sublime, landscape, wanderer

title: Mona Lisa
description_narrative: The most famous painting in the world. It depicts Lisa Gherardini, wife of Francesco del Giocondo, sitting in front of an imaginary landscape. Her enigmatic expression and the masterful use of sfumato make it a masterpiece of the Renaissance.
tags: portrait, renaissance, sfumato, enigmatic, female

title: Girl with a Pearl Earring
description_narrative: Often referred to as the 'Mona Lisa of the North', this tronie depicts a European girl wearing an exotic dress, an oriental turban, and what appears to be a very large pearl as an earring. It is a masterclass in the use of light.
tags: portrait, dutch golden age, pearl, turban, female

title: The Kiss
medium: Oil and gold leaf on canvas
description_narrative: A couple locked in an intimate embrace, cloaked in elaborate gold-patterned robes, kneeling in a flower-strewn meadow. Klimt's masterful use of gold leaf and ornamental motifs epitomizes his 'Golden Phase' and the height of the Vienna Secession movement.
tags: love, gold, art nouveau, couple, embrace, ornamental

title: The Night Watch
description_narrative: Rembrandt's most famous painting depicts a city guard led by Captain Frans Banninck Cocq. Revolutionary for its use of dramatic lighting, life-sized figures in motion, and the sense of spontaneous action in what was traditionally a static group portrait.
tags: group portrait, militia, baroque, dramatic lighting, dutch golden age

title: Water Lilies
description_narrative: Part of Monet's iconic series of approximately 250 oil paintings depicting his flower garden at Giverny. The water lily paintings collectively represent Monet's greatest artistic achievement, capturing the fleeting effects of light on water with unmatched mastery.
tags: water lilies, pond, impressionism, flowers, nature, reflections

title: The Birth of Venus
description_narrative: The goddess Venus emerges from the sea as a fully grown woman, arriving at the shore on a giant scallop shell. One of the world's most famous and appreciated works of art, it has become a landmark of Italian Renaissance painting.
tags: mythology, venus, renaissance, goddess, shell, classical

title: The Creation of Adam
description_narrative: The most famous section of the Sistine Chapel ceiling. God reaches out to give life to Adam, their fingers nearly touching. This single gesture has become one of the most replicated religious images and an enduring icon of humanity.
tags: fresco, sistine chapel, genesis, god, adam, renaissance

title: Impression, Sunrise
description_narrative: The painting that gave the Impressionist movement its name. A hazy sunrise over the port of Le Havre, rendered in loose, visible brushstrokes that prioritize the sensation of light and color over precise detail.
tags: sunrise, harbor, boats, impressionism, fog, water

title: Café Terrace at Night
description_narrative: A warmly lit café terrace on the Place du Forum in Arles, France, under a deep blue starry sky. One of Van Gogh's first paintings to feature his signature nocturnal sky, it contrasts the warm golden gaslight with the cool blues of the evening.
tags: cafe, night, stars, arles, post-impressionism, urban

title: The Scream
medium: Oil, tempera, pastel and crayon on cardboard
description_narrative: An agonized figure against a tumultuous orange sky, standing on a bridge. Widely interpreted as representing the universal anxiety of modern humanity. Munch described the inspiration as feeling an infinite scream passing through nature.
tags: scream, anxiety, expressionism, bridge, sunset, anguish

title: The Milkmaid
description_narrative: A kitchen maid carefully pours milk from a jug into an earthenware bowl, bathed in soft natural light from a window. Vermeer elevates an ordinary domestic scene into a meditation on quiet dignity and the beauty of everyday life.
tags: domestic, light, milk, kitchen, dutch golden age, still life

title: Sunflowers
description_narrative: One of two series of sunflower still lifes by Van Gogh. Painted in Arles to decorate the room of his friend Paul Gauguin, the vivid yellows and expressive brushwork make this among the most recognizable still-life paintings in art history.
tags: sunflowers, still life, yellow, vase, post-impressionism

title: The Dance Class
description_narrative: Young ballet dancers practice under the watchful eye of ballet master Jules Perrot in a rehearsal room at the Paris Opéra. Degas's masterful composition, with its off-center framing and casual poses, captures a fleeting moment with photographic spontaneity.
tags: ballet, dance, rehearsal, paris, impressionism, movement

title: American Gothic
description_narrative: A stern-faced farmer standing beside his spinster daughter in front of a house with a distinctive Gothic window. One of the most familiar images in 20th-century American art, it is an ambiguous statement about rural American character.
tags: portrait, american, gothic, rural, farmer, pitchfork

title: A Bar at the Folies-Bergère
description_narrative: Manet's last major work depicts a barmaid at the famous Parisian cabaret, standing behind a marble counter laden with bottles. The mirror behind her reflects the bustling crowd. A brilliant study in modernity and the complex relationship between viewer and subject.
tags: bar, barmaid, mirror, paris, impressionism, nightlife

title: Whistler's Mother
description_narrative: Formally titled 'Arrangement in Grey and Black No.1', this austere portrait of Whistler's mother seated in profile has become an American cultural icon and symbol of motherhood. Its restrained palette and compositional harmony exemplify Whistler's belief in art for art's sake.
tags: portrait, mother, seated, profile, tonalism, grey

title: The School of Athens
description_narrative: A fresco in the Apostolic Palace in the Vatican depicting a congregation of the greatest philosophers, mathematicians, and scientists of antiquity, with Plato and Aristotle at the center. It is considered a masterpiece of perspective and one of the greatest achievements of Renaissance art.
tags: philosophy, plato, aristotle, fresco, renaissance, architecture

title: Bal du moulin de la Galette
description_narrative: A vibrant depiction of a typical Sunday afternoon at the Moulin de la Galette in the Montmartre district of Paris. The dappled sunlight filtering through the trees onto the dancing crowd is one of Impressionism's most celebrated treatments of light and motion.
tags: dance, party, paris, montmartre, impressionism, sunlight

title: Nighthawks
description_narrative: Four figures sit in a brightly lit diner at night on a desolate city corner. The painting captures the loneliness and alienation of modern urban life with cinematic intensity. It has become one of the most reproduced paintings in American art.
tags: diner, night, urban, loneliness, realism, american

title: Liberty Leading the People
description_narrative: Commemorating the July Revolution of 1830, the allegorical figure of Liberty strides over barricades and fallen fighters, holding the tricolour flag aloft. It combines the idealism of classical art with the gritty reality of modern war.
tags: revolution, liberty, flag, france, romanticism, battle

title: Self-Portrait with Straw Hat
description_narrative: One of over thirty self-portraits by Van Gogh, painted during his time in Paris. The vibrant, short brushstrokes and complementary colors show the influence of the Impressionists and Neo-Impressionists he encountered in the French capital.
tags: self-portrait, hat, post-impressionism, portrait, paris
